Rule b: [Q][>]→[Q][U][>] (already bidirectional: adds U going forward, removes U going backward). Rule c: [F]→[V] (already bidirectional). Rule d: [>]→[I][E][M][E][>] (already bidirectional: adds suffix forward, removes it backward). Rule e: [<][U][N][>]→[<][P][R][E][M][I][E] (bidirectional with strict <UN> pattern so it only applies to standalone UN, not -UN> in compounds). For VINGT-ET-UN: The [<] requirement in rule e ensures it only matches standalone <UN>, not -UN>, so compounds correctly become VINGT-ET-UNIÈME in forward direction and reverse properly. For QUATRE-VINGTS: Rule a specifically targets [T][S] pattern, so VINGTS→VINGT forward and VINGT→VINGTS backward, yielding QUATRE-VINGTIÈME forward and QUATRE-VINGTS backward.
